_comment: Language-neutral conformance vectors for a future Rust port. Each value was computed by running the current Python implementation. See tests/test_conformance_vectors.py, which loads this file and asserts the code reproduces every vector.

auth_proof._doc: handshake._proof(secret, server_nonce, client_nonce, tag) = hmac_sha256(secret, b'nautilus-auth-v1' + server_nonce + client_nonce + tag). Inputs are hex; proof_hex is the 32-byte digest hex.

partition_stable_bucket._doc: stable_bucket(key, num_downstream): packb_hex = msgpack.packb(list(key), use_bin_type=True).hex(); digest_u64 = int.from_bytes(blake2b(packb, digest_size=8).digest(), 'big'); bucket_mod_4 = digest_u64 % 4. Key elements are tagged [type, value]; bytes value is hex, bool is distinct from int.

structural_digest._doc: report.structural_digest of a hand-built two-operator report (src->snk, Forward edge). See test for the exact snapshot the golden was derived from. Depends only on topology + STRUCTURAL_METRICS counters (rows_in/rows_out/eos.received), not timing/run_id.

transport_frame._doc: Wire framing. kind bytes are the single leading byte of a message. control_payload_hex is the msgpack payload; frame_hex is the full [kind][4-byte big-endian length][payload].
